role: Luca Faloni hiring Analytics Engineer in Greater Milan Metropolitan Area | Linke

evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement automated data quality tests to ensure trust and reliability.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD balances quality/reliability/testing language with delivery-focused responsibilities (build dashboards, translate business needs) roughly equally, landing on mixed.
evidence.domain_risk: LUCA FALONI is dedicated to delivering high-quality, timeless luxury fashion
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: A luxury fashion retailer's analytics work supports commercial/business decisions (Retail, Finance) without regulatory stakes, a moderate business-decision context.
evidence.data_team_maturity: We are seeking a talented Analytics Engineer to design, build, and maintain scalable data solutions that empower our business teams.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: No existing data team is named and the JD implies the candidate will build core data models/dashboards for the business largely independently, consistent with an early-stage data function at a small luxury retailer, though the JD does not use explicit first-hire language, so mid is used as a moderate reading of a newly forming function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Proficiency with dbt (data build tool) and modern cloud data warehouses (e.g., BigQuery). Experience defining and maintaining semantic layers.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD names specific tools and technical concepts (dbt, BigQuery, semantic layers, Fivetran/Census) a hiring manager would specify, mixed with generic "why join us" boilerplate.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Translate business needs from non-technical stakeholders (e.g., Retail, Finance) into robust technical requirements.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Retail and Finance are named as roughly co-equal stakeholder groups with no single one dominant, so mixed applies.
evidence.autonomy_level: Design, build, and maintain scalable data models.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Responsibilities are framed as delivering and maintaining defined data products (models, dashboards, tests) rather than setting overall data strategy or direction.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, build, and maintain scalable data models. Transform raw data into clean, reliable datasets for business stakeholders.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The JD describes both building new data models/dashboards and maintaining/ensuring quality of existing datasets, a mixed greenfield-and-maintenance scope.
evidence.ai_role: Basic understanding of machine learning concepts.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: ML/AI appears only as a vague preferred qualification, not a core candidate skill or infrastructure responsibility.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement automated data quality tests to ensure trust and reliability.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Candidate is directly responsible for implementing automated data quality tests, an ownership verb.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement automated data quality tests to ensure trust and reliability.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and trust language recurs but centers on dashboard/data accuracy rather than compliance or regulatory exposure.
